type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError and AttributeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection. TextExtents is a closed typed value with six required Double fields; tuple subclassing maps to fields, components, checked indexing, value equality, and hashing. Python repr/eval behavior maps to derived structural Debug and typed construction.

static_api:
pub struct TextExtents {
  x_bearing : Double
  y_bearing : Double
  width : Double
  height : Double
  x_advance : Double
  y_advance : Double
} derive(Eq, Hash, @debug.Debug)
pub fn TextExtents::new(Double, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double) -> Self
pub fn TextExtents::components(Self) -> (Double, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double)
pub fn TextExtents::at(Self, Int) -> Double raise CairoError
test_type.adaptation: TextExtents is a closed MoonBit struct rather than a Python tuple subclass. Fields, components, equality, hashing, and checked index syntax preserve the useful value protocol.

static_api: pub fn Context::glyph_extents(Self, ArrayView[@glyph.Glyph]) -> TextExtents raise CairoError
pub fn Context::text_extents(Self, String) -> TextExtents raise CairoError
pub fn ScaledFont::glyph_extents(Self, ArrayView[@glyph.Glyph]) -> TextExtents raise CairoError
pub fn ScaledFont::text_extents(Self, String) -> TextExtents raise CairoError

runtime: src/tests/value/core/value_types_test.mbt::pure value types preserve pycairo numeric limits
